findings.issue: No list virtualization. TaskList renders all tasks in the DOM. With hundreds or thousands of tasks, this causes excessive DOM nodes and slow renders. No react-window, react-virtual, or @tanstack/virtual is used anywhere in the codebase.
findings.recommendation: Use @tanstack/react-virtual for the TaskList component to only render visible rows. This is the single biggest scalability bottleneck.

findings.issue: PluginContext.Provider value is a new object literal on every render. Unlike TaskContext and UndoContext which use useMemo, PluginContext creates a new value object every render, causing all consumers to re-render on every PluginProvider state change.
findings.recommendation: Wrap the value prop in useMemo, matching the pattern used by TaskContext (line 202) and UndoContext (line 81).

findings.issue: VoiceContext.Provider value is a new object literal on every render. Same issue as PluginContext — no useMemo on the context value. Every state change (isListening, isSpeaking, etc.) forces all VoiceContext consumers to re-render.
findings.recommendation: Wrap the value prop in useMemo with all state/callback dependencies.

findings.issue: No code splitting / lazy loading for views. All 17+ views (Inbox, Today, Upcoming, Calendar, Stats, Matrix, Board, DopamineMenu, AIChat, etc.) are eagerly imported in ViewRenderer.tsx. Only one view is shown at a time, but all code is loaded upfront.
findings.recommendation: Use React.lazy() + Suspense for each view. Only the current view's code should be loaded. This could reduce initial bundle size significantly.

findings.issue: Every task count change triggers 3 API calls. useEffect depends on taskCount (state.tasks.length), so adding/completing/deleting any single task triggers fetchProjects(), fetchTags(), and fetchBlockedTaskIds() — 3 unnecessary network round-trips on every task mutation.
findings.recommendation: Remove taskCount dependency. Fetch projects/tags/blockedIds only on mount and after AI data mutations. Use event-driven updates instead of polling on task count.

findings.issue: Redundant task filtering — visibleTasks, inboxTaskCount, and todayTaskCount all iterate state.tasks independently with overlapping filter logic. With 1000 tasks, 'today' view causes 4 separate full-array scans on every task state change (visibleTasks + inboxTaskCount + todayTaskCount + projectTaskCounts).
findings.recommendation: Compute all derived counts in a single useMemo pass over state.tasks. Return visibleTasks, inboxCount, todayCount, projectCounts, and completedCounts from one iteration.

findings.issue: PluginView polls server every 500ms for structured views. This creates 2 HTTP requests/second per open plugin view, parsing and setting state each time even if content hasn't changed.
findings.recommendation: Use a content hash or ETag to skip re-renders when content hasn't changed. Consider WebSocket or SSE for live updates instead of polling. At minimum, compare new content with previous before calling setContent.

findings.issue: Command filtering runs on every render without useMemo. The filter is not memoized and runs during render, including when unrelated state changes cause re-renders.
findings.recommendation: Wrap in useMemo with [commands, query] dependencies.

findings.issue: useEffect depends on 'filtered' array reference which changes on every render (since filtered is not memoized). This causes the scrollIntoView effect to run on every render.
findings.recommendation: Memoize the filtered array first, then this effect will only run when query or commands actually change.

findings.issue: SearchModal filters all tasks on every keystroke with no debounce. The useMemo recomputes on each character typed. For large task lists with long descriptions and many tags, this does a .toLowerCase().includes() on every task title + description + tag for every keystroke.
findings.recommendation: Add a debounced query state (200-300ms) so filtering only runs after the user pauses typing.

findings.issue: O(n*m) complexity in bulk operations — for each selected ID, does a .find() over all tasks. With 50 selected tasks and 1000 total, this is 50 linear scans. Repeated in handleBulkComplete, handleBulkDelete, handleBulkMoveToProject, and handleBulkAddTag.
findings.recommendation: Build a Map<string, Task> once from state.tasks, then look up by ID in O(1).
findings.code_snippet: const tasks = ids.map((id) => state.tasks.find((t) => t.id === id)).filter(Boolean) as Task[];

findings.issue: handleBulkAddTag does sequential API calls in a loop — one updateTask per selected task. With 20 selected tasks, this creates 20 serial network requests.
findings.recommendation: Use updateManyTasks or batch the operations. If tags must differ per task, at least use Promise.all for parallel execution.

findings.issue: TASK_ADDED action creates a new array on every add via spread. This is fine for correctness but combined with the single TaskContext causing all consumers to re-render, it means every task addition re-renders the entire app.
findings.recommendation: Consider splitting TaskContext into separate state (tasks) and dispatch (actions) contexts so components that only call actions don't re-render when state changes.

findings.issue: SettingsContext stores ~50 settings as individual string properties. Any setting change re-renders all consumers, including components that only care about unrelated settings (e.g., changing accent_color re-renders nudge logic).
findings.recommendation: Split into granular contexts (AppearanceSettings, FeatureSettings, NudgeSettings) or use a selector pattern to prevent unnecessary re-renders.

findings.issue: AIContext refreshes tasks on every AI chat response. The useEffect watches dataMutationCount and calls refreshTasks(), which replaces the entire task array, cascading re-renders across all task consumers.
findings.recommendation: Only refresh tasks when a tool call actually mutated task data. Use granular updates (TASK_ADDED/TASK_UPDATED) instead of full refreshTasks().

findings.issue: Completed view renders all completed tasks without pagination. For users with hundreds of completed tasks, this creates excessive DOM nodes and slow initial render.
findings.recommendation: Add pagination (e.g., 'load more' button) or use virtual scrolling. Show last 50 completed tasks by default.

findings.issue: Math.max(...dailyCounts.map(d => d.count), 1) called during render without useMemo. Spread into Math.max creates a temporary array. Minor but unnecessary computation on every render.
findings.recommendation: Compute maxCount inside the dailyCounts useMemo or wrap in its own useMemo.

findings.issue: Same issue as Completed.tsx — no pagination for cancelled tasks list.
findings.recommendation: Add pagination or virtual scrolling.

findings.issue: Default parameter value 'new Set()' creates a new Set on every render when builtinPluginIds is not passed. This defeats reference equality checks.
findings.recommendation: Use a module-level constant: const EMPTY_SET = new Set<string>(); and reference that as the default.

findings.issue: PluginContext polls status bar and panels every 30 seconds unconditionally, even when no plugins are active or the app tab is not visible. Combined with the un-memoized context value, each poll triggers consumer re-renders.
findings.recommendation: Use document.visibilityState to pause polling when the tab is not active. Skip polling when plugins list is empty.

findings.issue: Nudge evaluation runs on mount AND re-runs whenever tasks change (via the evaluate callback dependency), AND also runs on a 5-minute interval. The tasks dependency means it runs on every task mutation, which is redundant with the interval.
findings.recommendation: Remove the tasks dependency from the useEffect (keep it only in the evaluate callback). Let the interval handle periodic re-evaluation. Or debounce the task-change evaluation.

findings.issue: Inline style objects created per render: style={{ ...style, ...indentPadding }}. The spread creates a new object reference on every render, though React.memo on TaskItem mitigates this for unchanged tasks.
findings.recommendation: Use useMemo for the merged style object when style or indentPadding changes.

findings.issue: toDateKey(new Date()) called multiple times in the same render cycle across different useMemo blocks. Each call creates a new Date object.
findings.recommendation: Compute today = toDateKey(new Date()) once at the top of the hook and reference it in all useMemo blocks (already done for visibleTasks but not for todayTaskCount which computes it independently).

findings.issue: JSON.parse called during render for tool call results. If tool results are large, this is expensive on every re-render.
findings.recommendation: Memoize the parsed results with useMemo.

findings.issue: JSON.parse inside render without memoization. Parses tool result data on every render.
findings.recommendation: Wrap in useMemo keyed on result.data.

findings.issue: JSON.parse(args) called during render without memoization.
findings.recommendation: Wrap in useMemo keyed on args string.

findings.issue: Multiple handler functions do state.tasks.find() to look up a single task by ID. This is O(n) per lookup. Four handlers (handleToggleTask, handleDeleteTask, handleUpdateDueDate, handleMoveTask) each do a linear scan.
findings.recommendation: Create a memoized taskMap = new Map(state.tasks.map(t => [t.id, t])) and use O(1) lookups.

findings.issue: filterTasks does a linear scan with multiple condition checks. For the tag filter, it uses .some() inside .filter(), making it O(n*t) where t = tags per task.
findings.recommendation: For frequently-used filters (status, projectId), consider indexed lookup maps in the storage layer rather than post-fetch filtering. Minor concern unless task counts reach thousands.

findings.issue: refreshTasks() fetches ALL tasks from API with no filtering or pagination. api.listTasks() returns every task regardless of status. As the task count grows, this becomes the primary bottleneck.
findings.recommendation: Add server-side pagination or at minimum filter by status (exclude completed/cancelled from the default fetch). Load archived tasks on demand.

findings.issue: PluginBrowser has 4 sequential useEffect hooks that each trigger independent state updates on mount, causing multiple re-renders in rapid succession.
findings.recommendation: Combine the initial data fetching into a single useEffect that batches state updates.
